ead.archdesc:
======= RMA03338Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Department of Communications records24-10-3338Compiled by Laura FinkelDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

vet lifesThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:27:45 UTC.ENG12/07/2018

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Department of Communications records
Cornell University. College of Veterinary Medicine. Department of Communications
24-10-3338
9.5 cubic feet. 9.5 cubic feet.
ca. 1970-2011. Videocassettes, audio cassettes, press releases, tear sheets, publications, photographs, and ephemera. Audiovisual materials produced and collected by the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Department of Communications, primarily related to . Collection material in English 5073120
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Finkel
Date completed:
May 2004
EAD encoding:
Laura Finkel, May 2004
Date modified:
RMC Staff, March 2016
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Videocassettes, audio cassettes, press releases, tear sheets, publications, slides, photographs, and ephemera from the Communications Department of the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine, dating from ca. 1970-2002.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Audio and Video Cassettes Boxes: 1, 7 -11
Several audio and video cassettes of varying formats. Most of the video cassettes are copies of stories run on television news or science programs featuring Cornell veterinarians. Some include local news coverage of college events. The audio tapes include recordings of college events as well as a number of American Association for the Advancement of Science program tapes. There are also 8 video tapes of interviews with several former deans of the college and then-president Hunter Rawlings which were recorded in 2000 for a promotional "Millennium" video that was never completed.
Series II. Publicity Subject Files Boxes: 2 and 6
Press releases, tear sheets (news clippings), and miscellaneous publications containing references to college people, events and issues.
Series III. Photographs by Subject Boxes: 2 - 4 and 9
Photographs, mostly from University Photography, arranged by subject; contains some slides.
Series IV. Photographs by Department Boxes: 5 and 6
Negatives, contact sheets and some photos. Mostly portraits of faculty and staff arranged by department.
Series V. Ephemera Boxes: 7
2 medallions created for the college centennial in 1994.
General
See also the Veterinary College of Medicine Biomedical Communications Department records, #24-14-3352 and the Veterinary College of Medicine subject files and photographs, #24-4-3385.
Cite As:
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Department of Communications records #24-10-3338. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
The Communications Department of the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine is responsible for publicity and external relations for the college. The college was known as the New York State College of Veterinary Medicine until the late 1980s.
Restrictions on Use:
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access.
Videotapes. Photographs. Veterinary medicine -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. College of Veterinary Medicine -- : Dept. of Communications. New York State College of Veterinary Medicine. Department of Communications
